data.title: Manulife Financial Q4 core earnings beat; raises dividend, launches new buyback program
data.description:
Manulife Financial Corp. (NYSE:MFC) shares gained 1.4% in Wednesday after-hours trading, after delivering Q4 bottom line that exceeded the average analyst estimate and announcing a dividend hike and a new share buyback program.

Q4 core EPS of C$1.03 (US$0.72), topping the C$0.94 consensus, rose from C$1.00 in the prior quarter and C$0.92 in Q4 2023. Excluding the impact of global minimum taxes, core EPS was $3.97

Adjusted book value per common share climbed to C$37.02 from C$34.97 in Q3 and C$32.19 a year ago.

Global WAM net inflows were C$1.2B, compared with C$5.2B of net inflows in Q3 and C$1.3B of net outflows in the year-earlier period.

New business CSM accelerated to C$842M from C$759M in Q3 and C$626M in Q4 2023.

New business value of C$842M vs. C$843M in the previous quarter and C$630M in Q4 2023.

In conjunction with the quarterly results, Manulife (NYSE:MFC) said it's planning to launch a share buyback program enabling the purchase for cancellation of up to 51.5M of its common shares, representing about 3% of MFC's issued and outstanding common shares.

It also raised its quarterly dividend by $0.04, or 10%, to $0.44 per share, payable on and after March 19, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on March 5, 2025.

data.title: Asbury Automotive appoints Daniel Clara as COO
data.description:
• Asbury Automotive Group (NYSE:ABG) on Wednesday said Senior Vice President of Operations Daniel Clara has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer effective February 17.
  • Prior to his 5 years as SVP of Operations, Clara held positions as regional vice president, market director, general manager, other store-level department leadership roles, and store-level frontline retail positions at Asbury.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blue Owl Capital Corp. Q4 total investment income slips, missing consensus
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE:OBDC) turned in Q4 total investment income that fell short of Wall Street expectations. The business development company had pre-announced Q4 net investment income of $0.47 last month and net asset value per share of $15.26 at Dec. 31, 2024, which slipped from $15.28 at Sept. 30.

Total investment income of $394.4M, lagging the $395.2M consensus, declined from $406.0M in the prior quarter and $411.2M in the year-ago period.

Q4 net investment income per share of $0.47, vs. the average analyst estimate of $0.46, was unchanged from Q3 and fell from $0.51 in Q4 2023.

The decline in NAV per share during the quarter reflected unrealized losses from credit marks in the portfolio, partially offset by over-earning of the base dividend.

Total investments at fair value slipped to $13.2B at the end of Q4 from $13.5B at the end of Q3.

New investment commitments during the quarter totaled $1.7B, offset by $1.6B of sales and repayments, compared with $1.2B of new investment commitments and $1.1B of sales and repayments in the prior quarter.

"Our portfolio has demonstrated resilience and stable credit quality over the past year, and we expect to build on our momentum in 2025 by leveraging the benefits of enhanced scale resulting from our completed merger with OBDE," said CEO Craig W. Packer.

After Q4-end Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE:OBDC) completed its merger with Blue Owl Capital Corp. III (OBDE), with OBDC as the surviving company.

The company's board declared a Q1 regular dividend of $0.37 a share for stockholders of record as of March 31, 2025, payable on or before April 15, 2025. That board also declared a Q4 supplemental dividend of $0.05 per share for stockholders of record as of Feb. 28, 2025, payable on or before March 17, 2025.

Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC) stock fell 0.4% in Wednesday after-hours trading.

Conference call on Feb. 20 at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blue Owl Capital reports Q4 results

data.title: NerdWallet GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.50, revenue of $183.8M beats by $15.46M
data.description:
• NerdWallet press release (NASDAQ:NRDS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.50.
  • Revenue of $183.8M (+37.5% Y/Y) beats by $15.46M.
  • Shares +10.16% AH.

  • FINANCIAL OUTLOOK

    We are providing guidance for the first quarter of 2025:

    • Revenue is expected in the range of $187-$193 million, up 17% year-over-year at the midpoint, Q1 revenue consensus of $178.80M
    • GAAP operating loss is expected in the range of $(12)-$(8) million
    • Non-GAAP operating income (loss) is expected in the range of $(3)-$0 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected in the range of $10-$13 million

data.title: Vimeo Holdings GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02, revenue of $103.16M beats by $2.57M
data.description:
• Vimeo Holdings press release (NASDAQ:VMEO): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $103.16M (-2.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.57M.
  • Shares -9.88% AH.
  • 2025 Outlook Demand for video is at an all-time high, and customers want more choices in how it is discovered, delivered, and monetized. Vimeo hosts over 8 billion video minutes and serves over 100 billion views annually. We are now in a strong position to increase investment and pursue accelerating growth profitably. In 2025, we will increase innovation investment in the key areas I mentioned above, utilizing shareholdersʼ resources wisely to accelerate profitable growth in bookings and revenue. We believe that as a result of these investments our growth rates will accelerate as we move through 2025, first in bookings and followed by revenue due to the subscription nature of our business. Vimeo Enterprise and OTT/streaming are delivering meaningful growth and in Q4, together, are now 38% of our bookings and 34% of our revenue. We are seeing positive early impacts of Self-Serve price increases. We have set goals to expand and delight our customer base across even more video use cases and believe if we achieve our goals, we can leave 2025 with double-digit growth in sight. On behalf of the entire Vimeo team, I extend our heartfelt thanks to you, our shareholders, for your belief in our vision for Vimeoʼs future. Customers need Vimeoʼs trust and innovation now more than ever, and we are committed to delivering just that. We are excited about the year ahead and look forward to sharing our progress with you.

data.title: Ferroglobe Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.02, revenue of $367.51M misses by $10.09M
data.description:
• Ferroglobe press release (NASDAQ:GSM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $367.51M (-2.2% Y/Y) misses by $10.09M.
  • Introducing 2025 Adj. EBITDA guidance of $100-$170 million
  • Posted adjusted EBITDA of $153.8 million for the full year 2024 and $9.8 million for the fourth quarter
  • Generated $164.1 million of free cash flow for the full year and $14.1 million for the fourth quarter
  • Trade measures to ensure fair trade in the U.S. and Europe are progressing well
  • Increasing quarterly dividend by 7.7% to $0.014 per share
  • Paid quarterly cash dividend of $0.013 per share in December
  • Repurchased approximately 482,000 shares during the fourth quarter.

data.title: Rollins announces pricing of $500M of 5.25% senior notes due 2035
data.description:
• Rollins (NYSE:ROL) on Wednesday said it has priced $500M aggregate principal amount of its 5.25% Senior Notes due 2035.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 24, 2025. The notes will mature on February 24, 2035.
  • Source: Press Release
